content.problemStatement:
For a fixed **binary** matrix BBB of size M×NM \times NM×N, hErd calls a **binary** array AAA of size NNN lucky if:


∑j=1NB(i,j)Aj≡0(mod2)\displaystyle{\sum_{j=1}^{N} B_{(i,j)} A_{j} \equiv 0 \pmod{2}}j=1∑N​B(i,j)​Aj​≡0(mod2)  for all 1≤i≤M1 \leq i \leq M1≤i≤M
The matrix BBB is constructed by setting B(i,j) (1≤i≤M,1≤j≤N)B_{(i,j)} \ (1 \leq i \leq M,  1 \leq j \leq N)B(i,j)​ (1≤i≤M,1≤j≤N) as 111 with probability p (0<p≤1)p \ (0 \lt p \leq 1)p (0<p≤1). Note that input for ppp is given in format XY\frac{X}{Y}YX​ where X≤YX \leq YX≤Y.

For a positive integer sss, let EsE_{s}Es​ be the expected number of lucky arrays AAA such that ∑i=1NAi=s\sum_{i=1}^{N} A_{i}=s∑i=1N​Ai​=s.

Given integer KKK, find the value ∑s=1KEs\sum_{s=1}^{K} E_{s}∑s=1K​Es​.

It can be shown that the answer can be represented as PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP​, where PPP and QQQ are coprime integers and Q≢0(mod109+7)Q \not\equiv 0 \pmod{10^9+7}Q≡0(mod109+7). Print the value of P⋅Q−1(mod109+7)P \cdot Q^{-1} \pmod{10^9+7}P⋅Q−1(mod109+7).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
First and only line of each test contains 555 space-separated integers N,M,K,X,YN, M, K, X, YN,M,K,X,Y, as mentioned in the statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the value ∑s=1KEs\sum_{s=1}^{K} E_{s}∑s=1K​Es​.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.
The sum of MMM over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.
The sum of KKK over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The array AAA has size 111, and matrix BBB has size 1×11\times 11×1.
Probability of B(i,j)=1B_{(i,j)} = 1B(i,j)​=1 is 12\frac{1}{2}21​. Therefore, the expected number of lucky arrays for s=1s = 1s=1 is 12=500000004mod  (109+7)\frac{1}{2} = 500000004 \mod (10^9 + 7)21​=500000004mod(109+7).
For  s=2s = 2s=2, the expected number of lucky arrays is 000 because the sum can never be 222.
Hence, the final answer is 500000004500000004500000004.
